title: oxalis quadriglandula
intro: Oxalis quadriglandula, commonly known as the four-angled wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its small, white flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Oxalis quadriglandula include its ability to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being. Oxalis quadriglandula can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
